tasks.description: Negligent Monitoring Risk Agent assesses co-defendant exposure. Privilege reshuffles across cases. DEPLOY-TIME PLACEHOLDER — agent entityKey + input/output schemas resolve when the negligent-monitoring-risk-agent is published; bindings carry best-effort resourceKey/name only.

action.messageTemplate: =js:`Litigation Defense SLA breached (case ${metadata.caseId}). Immediate action required.`

data.label: Fiduciary Review

tasks.description: HUMAN APPROVAL REQUIRED (HITL gate): Apex Health Plan demands direct provider data access. Legal review required. The fiduciary-conflict-detector agent produces this gate's payload (the multi-party obligation-conflict analysis presented for human approval). Reviewer context is captured into data.outputs[] (ReviewerId, ReviewerDecision, ReviewerContext, ReviewTimestamp) for the post-HITL stage to read.

data.taskTitle: Tri-Party Fiduciary Conflict: Apex vs ClearFlow vs Provider BAAs

inputs.name: PayerDemand
inputs.value: Apex Health Plan invokes the operational-visibility clause of its network-pricing agreement with ClearFlow Health Network, demanding immediate access to provider-level claim telemetry and underlying claim records for Northstar Regional Health and Provider Alpha. Apex asserts this supersedes ClearFlow confidentiality obligations and threatens to withhold scheduled remittances if access is not granted within 72 hours.

inputs.name: AffectedProviderBAAs
inputs.value: Northstar Regional Health BAA: prohibits redisclosure of claim-level PHI to any third party absent written provider authorization. Provider Alpha BAA: bars disclosure outside the treatment-payment-operations chain without a separate BAA; obligates ClearFlow to notify Provider Alpha within 24 hours of any payer demand. Provider Beta BAA: incorporates HIPAA 45 CFR 164.502(e) requiring downstream-recipient BAAs before disclosure — Apex holds no BAA with ClearFlow.

inputs.name: ClearFlowObligations
inputs.value: ClearFlow is bound by provider BAAs to act as a faithful business associate and disclose claim PHI only as those agreements permit. Cyber insurer Aurora Specialty has issued a reservation-of-rights directive instructing ClearFlow to freeze all voluntary, non-compelled disclosures of incident-related data until Northwall Forensics closes its investigation, to preserve coverage and privilege.

inputs.name: ConflictAnalysis
inputs.value: CONFLICT DETECTED — Three-way obligation collision: (1) Apex contractual demand vs. provider BAA confidentiality terms, (2) provider BAA redisclosure bar vs. payer operational-visibility clause, (3) insurer freeze directive vs. Apex 72-hour ultimatum. Complying with Apex violates at least two provider BAAs and the insurer directive. Recommended: grant time-boxed, scoped, audited access with minimum-necessary controls and written provider authorization.

data.name: =bindings.bCCAppNm1

data.description: Reversal 4 (Day 45): Tri-party fiduciary conflict HITL gate.

action.messageTemplate: =js:`Fiduciary Review SLA breached (case ${metadata.caseId}). Immediate action required.`

data.label: Regulatory Response

tasks.description: BAA Boundary Reasoner analyzes 6 BAA term sets. TN DOI subpoena triggers 6 stakeholder-parent spawns (the Reversal-3 fan). DEPLOY-TIME PLACEHOLDER — agent entityKey + input/output schemas resolve when the baa-boundary-reasoner is published; bindings carry best-effort resourceKey/name only.

inputs.name: provider_id
inputs.description: The single provider whose BAA terms are being analyzed (e.g. northstar, alpha, beta, gamma, delta, epsilon).

inputs.name: subpoena_scope
inputs.description: The scope of the regulatory subpoena being assessed against this provider's BAA.
inputs.default: Tennessee Department of Insurance subpoena (Reversal 3) compelling ClearFlow Health Network to produce 90 days of claim-flow records, payment-routing logs, and anomaly telemetry for Northstar Regional Health, including claim metadata and transaction identifiers that may constitute PHI. The request also reaches records touching the Nimbus Patient Engagement Platform subcontractor integration and asks whether any breach or incident affected potentially exposed PHI.

inputs.name: subpoena_template_id
inputs.description: The RegulatorTemplate identifier that defines the subpoena's discovery requests.

outputs.name: disclosure_position
outputs.description: ClearFlow's disclosure position for this provider (maps to the BAADisclosurePosition case variable).

_jsonSchema.description: Other providers whose BAA terms conflict with this provider's recommended action, each with a one-line reason.

body.description: Other providers whose BAA terms conflict with this provider's recommended action, each with a one-line reason.

outputs.name: rationale
outputs.description: Decision-support draft: BAA terms summary, disclosure obligation, and legal-risk note for counsel review.

tasks.displayName: Spawn Stakeholder Parent — Northstar Regional Health
tasks.description: Reversal-3 fan (1/6). Spawns a clearflow-stakeholder-parent case for provider 'northstar'. qem:Provider[slug='northstar'] — Data Fabric entity ref wired — entityKey + input/output schemas resolve when clearflow-stakeholder-parent is published. Intended inputs: StakeholderId='northstar', MasterCaseId=back-ref to this master case.

Reversal-3 fan (2/6). Spawns a clearflow-stakeholder-parent case for provider 'alpha'. qem:Provider[slug='alpha'] — Data Fabric entity ref wired — entityKey + input/output schemas resolve when clearflow-stakeholder-parent is published. Intended inputs: StakeholderId='alpha', MasterCaseId=back-ref to this master case.

Reversal-3 fan (3/6). Spawns a clearflow-stakeholder-parent case for provider 'beta'. qem:Provider[slug='beta'] — Data Fabric entity ref wired — entityKey + input/output schemas resolve when clearflow-stakeholder-parent is published. Intended inputs: StakeholderId='beta', MasterCaseId=back-ref to this master case.

Reversal-3 fan (4/6). Spawns a clearflow-stakeholder-parent case for provider 'gamma'. qem:Provider[slug='gamma'] — Data Fabric entity ref wired — entityKey + input/output schemas resolve when clearflow-stakeholder-parent is published. Intended inputs: StakeholderId='gamma', MasterCaseId=back-ref to this master case.

Reversal-3 fan (5/6). Spawns a clearflow-stakeholder-parent case for provider 'delta'. qem:Provider[slug='delta'] — Data Fabric entity ref wired — entityKey + input/output schemas resolve when clearflow-stakeholder-parent is published. Intended inputs: StakeholderId='delta', MasterCaseId=back-ref to this master case.

Reversal-3 fan (6/6). Spawns a clearflow-stakeholder-parent case for provider 'epsilon'. qem:Provider[slug='epsilon'] — Data Fabric entity ref wired — entityKey + input/output schemas resolve when clearflow-stakeholder-parent is published. Intended inputs: StakeholderId='epsilon', MasterCaseId=back-ref to this master case.

data.label: Vector Isolation

tasks.description: Forensic Self-Exam Agent clears ClearFlow. Vector Hypothesis Agent identifies Nimbus. DEPLOY-TIME PLACEHOLDER — agent entityKey + input/output schemas resolve when the vector-hypothesis-agent is published; bindings carry best-effort resourceKey/name only.

Forensic Self-Exam Agent coordinates ClearFlow's internal investigation and produces the vector determination that clears ClearFlow. DEPLOY-TIME PLACEHOLDER — agent entityKey + input/output schemas resolve when the forensic-self-exam-agent is published; bindings carry best-effort resourceKey/name only.

data.description: Reversal 2 (Day 5): ClearFlow cleared. Nimbus identified as vector.

exitConditions.displayName: ClearFlow cleared by Forensic Self-Exam Agent

action.messageTemplate: =js:`Vector Isolation SLA breached (case ${metadata.caseId}). Immediate action required.`

data.label: Multi-Customer Investigation

tasks.description: Claim Flow Anomaly Detector classifies anomaly scores on per-provider claim telemetry. Reversal 1 setup. DEPLOY-TIME PLACEHOLDER — agent entityKey + input/output schemas resolve when the claim-flow-anomaly-detector is published; bindings carry best-effort resourceKey/name only.

Multi-Customer Pattern Detector correlates anomalous claim drops across providers and emits the cascade signal that confirms the Reversal 1 trigger. DEPLOY-TIME PLACEHOLDER — agent entityKey + input/output schemas resolve when the multi-customer-pattern-detector is published; bindings carry best-effort resourceKey/name only. shouldRunOnlyOnce:false — this is the SELECTED task that re-runs on the Reversal 5 targeted re-entry (the settled anomaly classification stays run-once).

data.description: Reversal 1 (Day 1): Claim drops correlate across providers.

action.messageTemplate: =js:`Multi-Customer Investigation SLA breached (case ${metadata.caseId}). Immediate action required.`

tasks.description: Initial assessment: the Claim Flow Anomaly Detector reviews claim telemetry from all 6 provider systems and classifies per-provider anomaly. Auto-runs on stage entry and completes on return, so Initial Response self-advances. Bound to the deployed claim-flow-anomaly-detector (reuses the Stage-2 binding).

action.messageTemplate: =js:`Initial Response SLA breached (case ${metadata.caseId}). Immediate action required.`

data.label: Reversal 4: Payer demands BAA data access
